notes: DELAYED-ONSET / INNOCENT-DEPLOY-AT-ONSET (hard). Culprit c6566fc was deployed to recommendationservice at 08:25 and its diff (services/recommendation/history.go) appends a scoreRecord to a package-level slice `scoreLog` on every Score() call with no eviction/cap -> unbounded growth. RSS climbs steadily from ~216MB at 08:25 to ~500MB at 10:43 (limit 512), OOMKilled at 10:44 (alert 10:44:30) -- ~2h19m after the deploy. The TEMPTING wrong answer is f72cb9d (prefetch goroutine), the most-recent reco deploy at 10:32, only ~12min before the kill; but its diff uses a fixed-size LRU (trendingMax=256) and is memory-bounded, and RSS was already ~473MB and climbing for 2h before that deploy landed -> it cannot be the cause. Other surface-match decoys: 4bded17 (preallocate JSON encoder buffer) and 966b58e (sync.Pool reuse in encoder) touch memory/buffers but are bounded/innocent. flags.json reco_model_v3 off->on at 10:15 is a distractor (root cause is always a commit). Identify the leak by reading the diff that appends to an unbounded package-level container per request, then trace RSS back to the 08:25 deploy of that commit -- not the latest deploy. Remediation: roll back to before c6566fc.
